notes: Primary strategic platform for Digital's commercial desktop and server products; 64-bit NT development with Digital announced
Continuous computing server platform; positioned to complement NT, not compete; VMS on Alpha; still maintained by VSI as of 2026
64-bit Unix for high-end decision support and data warehouses; renamed Tru64 UNIX after Compaq acquisition
Ran all three DEC OSes; AlphaServer line from 1000 to 4100; 64-bit; manufactured at Hudson Fab
Binary translation/emulation enabling 32-bit Windows/NT x86 apps to run on Alpha with performance improvements
Integrated with DEC ClientWORKS for asset management; later became SCCM/MECM
Enterprise management framework; integrated with CA Unicenter via Alliance for Enterprise Management
Heterogeneous systems management; partnered with DEC POLYCENTER for NT enterprise management
Embedded in DEC Multia enterprise client for web access; sign of early enterprise Internet integration
